costs: Cost of statutory transport guarantee across dispersed, low-frequency rural transport regions
TEIP-style targeted support funding requires sustained budget commitment
Administrative cost of annual public data publication and threshold monitoring
content.equity_impact: Directly targets the equity harms most robustly documented in the evidence (transport burden, Roma/disadvantage concentration, achievement disruption) with dedicated compensatory mechanisms, but accepts closure as the operative default for sub-threshold schools, meaning community and demographic-momentum harms (F-live-international_comparison-01, F-live-demography-07) are not prevented, only their downstream pupil-level effects are mitigated.
expected_benefits: Provides predictability and legal clarity currently absent from ad hoc administrative closures
Directly addresses documented transport-related harms through a resolved statutory obligation rather than disputed responsibility
Creates a transparent, evidence-based trigger rather than opaque efficiency-ratio judgment calls
Builds in anti-segregation review responsive to ongoing EU infringement and ECtHR findings (F-live-portuguese_reform-09)
content.goal: Where village schools become genuinely unviable under transparent minimum-enrollment criteria, close them by default while guaranteeing statutory transport, cost transparency, and targeted compensatory support to offset travel burden and segregation risk for affected pupils.

implementation_steps.action: Enact statute definitively assigning pupil-transport obligation (resolving current ombudsman-flagged ambiguity) and defining a transparent minimum-viability threshold formula
Publish enrollment, cost, and threshold data for at-risk schools annually, triggering consolidation review only when threshold is breached for a defined consecutive period
Establish TEIP-style targeted support funding and transition-support programs (counseling, induction) for displaced pupils at consolidated receiving schools
Review each proposed consolidation for segregation risk before finalization, per Szolcsán v. Hungary obligations
implementation_steps.actor: Non-discrimination oversight body (linked to ECtHR/EU infringement compliance)

mechanisms: Legal responsibility for pupil transport to consolidated schools is currently disputed between the Interior Ministry and municipalities, with an ombudsman inquiry finding no clear statutory assignment—this gap must be closed to make consolidation defensible (F-live-legal_and_governance-05).

Rural school bus commutes averaging over 30 minutes are associated with skipped breakfast, lower grades, poorer fitness, and fewer social activities, disproportionately burdening lower-income households, indicating mitigation must go beyond simple transport provision to address these downstream harms (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10).

A quasi-experimental Finnish study isolated a measurable short-term achievement disruption from consolidation itself (independent of prior school quality) affecting displaced students, indicating need for transition support distinct from general disadvantage funding (F-live-finnish_reform-06).

Consolidation/closure removes local options most accessible to poorer and Roma families with lower mobility, and Roma children constitute 45% of students in segregated schools/classrooms nationally, requiring explicit anti-segregation safeguards tied to any closure (F-live-education_finance-06).

Free public transport for under-14s introduced in 2023 provides a partial existing infrastructure base, but rural transport frequency remains limited in the same regions facing highest depopulation (F-live-implementation_planning-09).
risks: Even with mitigation, Danish and Finnish evidence suggests closures may accelerate local depopulation via social capital and housing-market lock-in mechanisms, an effect mitigation measures may not offset (F-live-demography-07, F-live-international_comparison-01)

Contested evidence on consolidation cost savings (Texas study found none; NY study found substantial savings) means fiscal rationale for the underlying threshold-triggered closures is uncertain (F-live-education_finance-02)
Community hub loss and its second-order effects on rural viability are not addressed by transport/funding mitigation alone
content.title: Consolidation-with-mitigation: default closure paired with statutory transport, funding transparency, and targeted equity support
